purpose: Source-anchored, proof-level modern reconstructions of the target theorem families in all 21 unique source papers.

AdjointMatching2025_memoryless_soc.tex.scope: Sections 2--5 and Appendices B.1--B.4, C.4, D.1--D.2, and E.1--E.3: the unified Flow-Matching/DDIM sampler, forward/backward schedule invariance, the memoryless-noise characterization, the SOC fine-tuning theorem, the continuous-adjoint identity, and critical-point uniqueness for basic and lean Adjoint Matching.
anchors: Section 4, equations (16)--(26), Definition 1, Proposition 1, and Theorem 1
Appendix B.1--B.4, equations (44)--(109), Lemmas 1--2, and Propositions 3--4
Appendix E.1--E.3, equations (180)--(218), Proposition 6, Lemmas 5--6, and Propositions 7--8
AdjointMatching2025_memoryless_soc.tex.status: Proof-level source-order reconstruction completed in the 2026-07-10 single-document pass.
source_issues_recorded: Proposition 4 is derived through a discrete Euler--Maruyama approximation and is explicitly described by the source as not completely formal.
The necessity direction of Proposition 1 is argued only in a generic/heuristic sense.
The terminal sign printed in source equation (166) conflicts with equations (165), (167), and the target tilt.
Source equations (216)--(218) switch from X^v to X^* and omit the control Jacobian unless the control is state-independent.
Classical PDE, differentiation, and conditional-Gronwall steps require regularity and integrability assumptions not collected in the source theorem statements.

source_files: Birkhoff 1957 - Extensions of Jentzsch theorem - S0002-9947-1957-0087058-6.pdf
Birkhoff1957_extensions_jentzsch.tex.scope: Sections 2--8: the line calculation, Hilbert metric on cones, exact finite-diameter contraction coefficient, fixed-ray theorem, positive matrix and integral-operator applications, uniformly positive Banach-lattice spectral gap, complementary invariant subspace, and multiplicative semigroups.
Birkhoff1957_extensions_jentzsch.tex.status: Proof-level source-order reconstruction completed in the 2026-07-10 three-paper pass.
source_issues_recorded: The general Hilbert-metric lemma is cited as well known rather than proved in the source.
The fixed-point statement is projective; a scalar normalization is needed to write cP=c rather than cP=gamma c.
Theorem 4 uses the historical phrase spectral norm for an asymptotic spectral-gap estimate.
The scanned typography in the Theorem 2 ratio estimate is ambiguous; the reconstruction preserves the inequalities sufficient for cone invariance.

BlanchetMurthy2019_model_risk_ot.tex.scope: Theorem 1 and its full Section 4 proof: compact Fenchel duality, monotone approximation of lower-semicontinuous costs, sigma-compact localization and stitching, Sion minimax, Jankov--von Neumann near-maximizers, scalar dual attainment, and complementary slackness; plus Proposition 9 and Corollary 1 on primal attainment.
BlanchetMurthy2019_model_risk_ot.tex.status: Proof-level source-order reconstruction completed in the 2026-07-10 three-paper pass.
source_issues_recorded: The dual majorant is universally measurable through analytic-set projection; Borel measurability is not automatic.
The source uses an extended-real convention for primal objectives and explains the finite-negative-part reduction in a footnote.
Strong duality does not imply primal attainment on noncompact spaces; Proposition 9 adds separate compactness and upper-semicontinuity conditions.
Appendix Lemma 16 is printed with an unqualified limit, while its proof establishes the lower-limit inequality used in the main argument.

BlanchetMurthy2019_theorem1.tex.scope: Theorem 1 and its full Section 4 proof: compact Fenchel duality, monotone approximation of lower-semicontinuous costs, sigma-compact localization and stitching, Sion minimax, Jankov--von Neumann near-maximizers, scalar dual attainment, and complementary slackness; plus Proposition 9 and Corollary 1 on primal attainment.
BlanchetMurthy2019_theorem1.tex.status: Proof-level source-order reconstruction completed in the 2026-07-10 three-paper pass.
source_issues_recorded: The dual majorant is universally measurable through analytic-set projection; Borel measurability is not automatic.
The source uses an extended-real convention for primal objectives and explains the finite-negative-part reduction in a footnote.
Strong duality does not imply primal attainment on noncompact spaces; Proposition 9 adds separate compactness and upper-semicontinuity conditions.
Appendix Lemma 16 is printed with an unqualified limit, while its proof establishes the lower-limit inequality used in the main argument.

CGP2021_sinkhorn_schrodinger_bridge.tex.scope: Sections 5--8 core theorem chain: path-space entropy decomposition and dynamic/static reduction, Schrödinger factorization, Brownian and general-prior stochastic control, generalized HJB/Hopf--Cole verification, entropic optimal transport, the continuous Fortet fixed-point formulation, and the finite Hilbert-metric Sinkhorn theorem.
anchors: Sections 5.2--5.5 and equations decomposition, staticindex, representation, optimaljoint, opt1--BConestep, girsanov, eq:regularizedOT
Section 6 and equations FDProblem, Lagrangian2, prioroptcond, HJB, generalizedschrodinger, optlaw
Sections 8.1--8.4, Theorem Fundtheorem, Lemmas Linear, inversion, compothm
CGP2021_sinkhorn_schrodinger_bridge.tex.status: Proof-level source-order reconstruction completed in the 2026-07-10 three-paper pass.
source_issues_recorded: The entropy disintegration and Girsanov formulas are cited rather than proved in the survey.
The variational and PDE calculations are formal unless the required regularity and boundary decay are supplied.
The generalized Schrödinger theorem is a verification theorem and does not prove PDE existence.
The continuous Fortet subsection states and interprets Fortet’s theorem but does not reproduce Fortet’s three-sequence proof.
The discrete proof first obtains a fixed ray and then uses total-mass conservation to show the ray multiplier is one; raw-vector convergence requires normalization control.
The source contains a repeated/incorrect discrete-system cross-reference.

CameronMartin1945_wiener_transformations.scope: Theorem I, Theorem Ia, Theorem II, the polygonal-kernel reduction, convergence lemmas, and the final quadratic-functional example.
CameronMartin1945_wiener_transformations.status: Proof-level source-order reconstruction completed in the 2026-07-10 three-paper pass (batch 2).
source_issues_recorded: The source invokes the authors' earlier translation theorem rather than reproving it; that dependency is cited explicitly.
Several determinant and Stieltjes-limit passages are compressed in the source and are expanded without changing their approximation route.
The final quadratic-functional calculation depends on a branch choice for the square root of the secant factor.

Carroll2004_birkhoff_contraction.tex.scope: Theorem 1 and the complete finite-dimensional linear-programming reduction proving the sharp Birkhoff contraction coefficient.
Carroll2004_birkhoff_contraction.tex.status: Proof-level source-order reconstruction completed in the 2026-07-10 three-paper pass (batch 2).
source_issues_recorded: The paper presents a finite-dimensional linear-programming reduction and does not claim the argument for general cones.
The strict contraction statement is separated from the non-strict coefficient bound at the boundary cases.
The modern linear-programming terminology used to clarify the reduction is attributed separately from Carroll's proof.

source_files: Multiple classical sources; see the note bibliography and source map.
ClassicalFoundations_DV_KLconvexity_supe.scope: A normalized proof dossier for the Donsker--Varadhan variational identity, Gibbs attainment, KL convexity/data processing, and one-dimensional supporting-slope arguments.
ClassicalFoundations_DV_KLconvexity_supe.status: Multi-source synthesis maintained separately from the 21-paper completion count; its component arguments are source-anchored but it is not treated as a single paper reconstruction.
source_issues: This note combines several classical results and therefore has no single associated primary paper.

DSBM2023_diffusion_schrodinger_bridge_ma.scope: Iterative Markovian Fitting and its convergence: Markovian projection, reciprocal projection, KL Pythagorean identities, fixed-point identification, and the DSBM regression realization.
DSBM2023_diffusion_schrodinger_bridge_ma.status: Proof-level source-order reconstruction completed in the 2026-07-10 three-paper pass (batch 2).
source_issues_recorded: The convergence theorem relies on compactness of KL sublevel sets, lower semicontinuity, and closure of the Markov and reciprocal classes under the stated appendix assumptions.
The Pythagorean identities use exact conditional-law and Radon--Nikodym calculations; these are reconstructed rather than replaced by an abstract projection theorem.
The passage from vanishing successive KL increments to convergence of the entire alternating-projection sequence requires the compactness and fixed-point argument made explicit in the note.
Several stochastic-calculus identities are cited by the paper from standard Girsanov, time-reversal, and reciprocal-process references; those dependencies are named explicitly.

EvesonNussbaum1995_birkhoff_hopf.tex.scope: Complete reconstruction of the abstract Birkhoff--Hopf theorem: order preliminaries, the exact N and k identities, two-dimensional cone classification, normalization to the symmetric 2x2 problem, the infinite-diameter reduction, and exact matrix and integral-kernel cross-ratio formulas.
EvesonNussbaum1995_birkhoff_hopf.tex.status: Proof-level source-order reconstruction completed in the 2026-07-10 four-paper pass.
source_issues: The scanned source frequently renders the projective-diameter symbol Delta as an OCR letter A.
Lemma 3.8 is cited rather than proved in the source.
Theorem 6.3 is explicitly attributed to Hopf; the paper supplies the cross-ratio proof in its unified notation.

Fortet1940_schrodinger_system.tex.scope: Fortet’s complete existence and uniqueness route: reduction to one nonlinear equation, class (C), truncation and mass identity, the exact three-sequence clipped iteration and its two cases, class (B), Theorems I--III, and the Gaussian Bernstein application.
Fortet1940_schrodinger_system.tex.status: Proof-level source-order reconstruction completed in the 2026-07-10 four-paper pass.
source_issues: The source compresses the zero-exclusion and uniform tail-control argument in the second iteration case.
The source invokes Beppo--Levi completed by de la Vallée Poussin; the modern reconstruction separates monotone convergence from uniform-integrability claims.
Uniqueness is modulo the scalar gauge, fixed temporarily at a point in Fortet’s proof.

FranklinLorenz1989_matrix_scaling.tex.scope: Section 3 in full: alternating row/column normalization, Hilbert's metric, Birkhoff contraction (Lemma 1), the two-step contraction (Lemma 2), the diagonal-equivalence metric and geometric tail estimate (Theorem 4), entrywise error conversion (Lemma 3), equations (3.9) and (3.12), and both numerical examples.
FranklinLorenz1989_matrix_scaling.tex.status: Proof-level source-order reconstruction completed in the 2026-07-10 four-paper pass.
source_issues: The printed diameter symbol is easily confused with an additive projective diameter; the proof uses the multiplicative cross-ratio quantity in equation (3.4).
Theorem 4's printed proof invokes Sinkhorn's earlier convergence theorem, while its completeness note explains how the same estimates yield an independent Cauchy argument.
The examples compare the computable bound with the actual error and are retained to preserve the paper's quantitative conclusion.

GSBM2024_generalized_schrodinger_bridge_.scope: Complete reconstruction of the GSBM mathematical core: prescribed-path matching, the two alternating stages, conditional SOC, HJB/Hopf--Cole representation, the quadratic Gaussian bridge, path-integral reweighting, objective descent, fixed-point characterization, explicit Markovian projection, Gaussian joint sampling, and the deterministic LQR limit.
GSBM2024_generalized_schrodinger_bridge_.status: Proof-level source-order reconstruction completed in the 2026-07-10 four-paper pass.
source_issues: The final conditional Fokker--Planck display in the proof of Proposition 2 drops the factor sigma^2/2 although the preceding derivation and SDE retain it.
Equation (18) is ambiguous by a factor sigma^{-2} in the state-cost term; Proposition 4 later uses the scaled potential explicitly.

Proposition 4 uses a singleton endpoint indicator and absolute continuity of an exactly pinned proposal relative to unconditioned Brownian motion; in continuous state space this is formal bridge-conditioning shorthand.
Theorem 5 proves monotone objective descent, not convergence of iterates.
Theorem 6 relies on cited FBSDE/HJB uniqueness and Markovian-projection results.

GaoKleywegt2023_theorem1.tex.scope: Section 3 in full: Proposition 1, Definitions 3--4, Lemmas 2--7, Proposition 2, Theorem 1, Corollaries 1--2, including measurable near-minimizers, the interior and growth-boundary dual cases, exact worst-case-law criteria, and the empirical finite-support construction.
GaoKleywegt2023_theorem1.tex.status: Proof-level source-order reconstruction completed in the 2026-07-10 five-paper pass.
source_issues: The upper/lower D and D0 glyphs are visually ambiguous in the published PDF; the reconstruction uses +/- superscripts while retaining the definitions.
The lambda*=kappa boundary proof is essential and is preserved rather than replaced by an interiority argument.

GaoKleywegt2023_wasserstein_drso.tex.scope: Section 3 in full: Proposition 1, Definitions 3--4, Lemmas 2--7, Proposition 2, Theorem 1, Corollaries 1--2, including measurable near-minimizers, the interior and growth-boundary dual cases, exact worst-case-law criteria, and the empirical finite-support construction.
GaoKleywegt2023_wasserstein_drso.tex.status: Proof-level source-order reconstruction completed in the 2026-07-10 five-paper pass.
source_issues: The upper/lower D and D0 glyphs are visually ambiguous in the published PDF; the reconstruction uses +/- superscripts while retaining the definitions.
The lambda*=kappa boundary proof is essential and is preserved rather than replaced by an interiority argument.

Girsanov1960_change_of_measure.tex.scope: Sections 1--3: the conditional-moment Wiener characterization, exponential substitution, Lemmas 1--6 and Fundamental Theorem 1, Lemma 7 and Fundamental Theorem 2 for diffusion-type processes, plus a separately cited modern entropy consequence.
Girsanov1960_change_of_measure.tex.status: Proof-level source-order reconstruction completed in the 2026-07-10 five-paper pass.
source_issues: The translated scan loses several symbols in the theorem statements; signs and coefficients are reconstructed from the surrounding equations and proof.
The source assumes normalization of the exponential density and does not derive it from square-integrability alone.

Kakutani1948_product_measures.tex.scope: The supplied modern exposition in full: Theorem 2.3, Lemmas 2.4--2.8, Corollaries 2.10--2.12, and Cantor-space Propositions 3.2 and 3.5, with the original Kakutani theorem identified as the primary result.
Kakutani1948_product_measures.tex.status: Proof-level source-order reconstruction completed in the 2026-07-10 five-paper pass.
source_issues: The supplied exposition reverses the derivative orientation in one display near Corollary 2.12.
The Bernoulli affinity is printed once with a minus sign; the definition and following Hellinger identity require a plus sign.

LemmensNussbaum2013_hilbert_metric_surve.scope: Sections 2--4: cone-order Hilbert geometry, Birkhoff contraction, positive operators, special cones, periodic-orbit results, and Denjoy--Wolff-type boundary dynamics. Proofs supplied by the survey are expanded; quoted results retain their cited status.
LemmensNussbaum2013_hilbert_metric_surve.status: Proof-level source-order reconstruction completed in the 2026-07-10 five-paper pass.
source_issues: Most Section 4 theorems are quoted from external papers rather than proved in the survey; the reconstruction explicitly preserves that status.

Leonard2014_schrodinger_survey.tex.scope: Sections 2--5: sigma-finite entropy, dynamic/static reduction, existence and product-form theorems, Markov inheritance, (f,g)-transforms, Brownian and jump-process action principles, the slowing-down dual calculation, and Theorem 5.2.
Leonard2014_schrodinger_survey.tex.status: Proof-level source-order reconstruction completed in the 2026-07-10 five-paper pass.
source_issues: The general Section 5 convergence discussion is explicitly informal until supplemented by the cited Gamma-convergence results.
The product-form theorem requires internality and domination hypotheses; the survey warns that the non-internal case may have a more complicated support structure.

Leonard2019_revisiting_fortet.tex.scope: Sections 1--7 in source order: reduction of the Schrodinger system to Fortet's scalar equation, the extended-valued monotone scheme, mass identity, uniqueness, Theorem 5.6, compact-continuity/kernel-twist variant, L^r ratio variant, and Gaussian example.
Leonard2019_revisiting_fortet.tex.status: Proof-level source-order reconstruction completed in the 2026-07-10 final five-paper pass.
source_issues: Hypotheses 1.11 and 5.4 contain duplicated letter labels in the source; the note relabels without changing content.
The clipped floor U/(n+1) is retained because it is used at the decisive finite stopping stage, not treated as cosmetic regularization.

MohajerinEsfahaniKuhn2018_data_driven_wd.scope: Sections 3--4 and the supporting appendix arguments: concentration radius, finite-sample guarantee, asymptotic consistency, exact finite convex reformulation, perspective duality, and finite-atomic extremal distributions.
MohajerinEsfahaniKuhn2018_data_driven_wd.status: Proof-level source-order reconstruction completed in the 2026-07-10 final five-paper pass.
source_issues: The concentration theorem is cited by the paper rather than proved there; the note preserves that dependency.
The conjugate constraints depend on a global sign convention for the dual vector; the note fixes one equivalent convention throughout.

SinkhornKnopp1967_matrix_scaling.tex.scope: The complete six-page theorem: support and total support, full indecomposability, uniqueness of the doubly stochastic representative and scaling factors, Lemmas 1--2, alternating-normalization convergence, and the non-total-support limit.
SinkhornKnopp1967_matrix_scaling.tex.status: Proof-level source-order reconstruction completed in the 2026-07-10 final five-paper pass.
source_issues: The scan contains OCR damage in products and subscripts; the note normalizes these from the surrounding algebra.
The source proves convergence by product monotonicity and compactness, not by a projective contraction; that distinction is preserved.

WangGaoXie2025_sinkhorn_dro.tex.scope: Definition 1; equations (1)--(9); Assumption 1; Condition 1; Theorem 1(I)--(IV); Lemmas 1--3; Proposition EC.1; Lemmas EC.2--EC.5; positive-, zero-, and non-light-tailed multiplier cases; the worst-case-law formula; and the small-regularization comparison.
WangGaoXie2025_sinkhorn_dro.tex.status: Proof-level source-order reconstruction completed in the 2026-07-10 final five-paper pass.
source_issues: The source reuses rho for the shifted entropy budget; the notes write bar-rho.
The arXiv and final publication use different year labels; the notes cite the final 2025 publication.

The printed Theorem 1(III) appears to classify the shifted-radius boundary bar-rho = 0 as infinite, while the appendix proof gives the explicit baseline value; the notes preserve both and identify the conflict.
The appendix reference attached to the negative-radius convention appears to point to Lemma EC.4 even though that lemma concerns existence for positive radius.
The zero-mass contradiction chooses a level H^ell(x) pointwise and then integrates the resulting bound without a separate measurable-selection argument.
Minor dummy-variable and multiplier-subscript slips in the electronic companion are normalized without changing the proof route.

WangGaoXie2025_sinkhorn_dro_theorem1.tex.scope: Definition 1; equations (1)--(9); Assumption 1; Condition 1; Theorem 1(I)--(IV); Lemmas 1--3; Proposition EC.1; Lemmas EC.2--EC.5; positive-, zero-, and non-light-tailed multiplier cases; the worst-case-law formula; and the small-regularization comparison.
WangGaoXie2025_sinkhorn_dro_theorem1.tex.status: Proof-level source-order reconstruction completed in the 2026-07-10 final five-paper pass.
source_issues: The source reuses rho for the shifted entropy budget; the notes write bar-rho.
The arXiv and final publication use different year labels; the notes cite the final 2025 publication.

The printed Theorem 1(III) appears to classify the shifted-radius boundary bar-rho = 0 as infinite, while the appendix proof gives the explicit baseline value; the notes preserve both and identify the conflict.
The appendix reference attached to the negative-radius convention appears to point to Lemma EC.4 even though that lemma concerns existence for positive radius.
The zero-mass contradiction chooses a level H^ell(x) pointwise and then integrates the resulting bound without a separate measurable-selection argument.
Minor dummy-variable and multiplier-subscript slips in the electronic companion are normalized without changing the proof route.

source_files: Transformations of Wiener Integrals Under Translations-Yeh-1977.pdf
Yeh1978_conditional_wiener_translation.t.scope: Section 1 equations (1.1)--(1.6); Section 2 definition, Lemmas 1--2, and Theorem 1 through equation (2.19); Section 3 Cameron--Martin specialization, Theorem 2, corollary, and the final Paley--Wiener endpoint integral through equation (3.12).
anchors: Example, equations (3.11)--(3.12), and final endpoint integral
Yeh1978_conditional_wiener_translation.t.status: Proof-level source-order reconstruction completed in the 2026-07-10 final five-paper pass.
source_issues: The archive filename says 1977 while the journal volume is 1978; the note follows the published citation.
Yeh cites the 1944 Cameron--Martin translation paper, not the different 1945 general-linear-transformation paper.
The corollary explicitly transports endpoint null sets because conditional expectations are equivalence classes.
The source distinguishes the bounded-variation Riemann--Stieltjes integral from the L2 Paley--Wiener--Zygmund integral; the note retains that distinction.

paper_progress.counting_rule: Unique associated source papers; theorem-focused companion notes and the multi-source classical synthesis are not counted separately.
